sentences.target: Ich habe ein Problem mit meinem Handy.
sentences.reading: /ɪç ˈhaːbə aɪn pʁoˈbleːm mɪt ˈmaɪnəm ˈhɛndi/

translations.de: Ich habe ein Problem mit meinem Handy.
translations.ipa: /ɪç ˈhaːbə aɪn pʁoˈbleːm mɪt ˈmaɪnəm ˈhɛndi/
translations.en: I have a problem with my phone.
translations.es: Tengo un problema con mi teléfono.
translations.fr: J’ai un problème avec mon téléphone.
translations.it: Ho un problema con il mio telefono.
translations.pt: Tenho um problema com o meu telefone.
translations.he: יש לי בעיה עם הטלפון שלי.
translations.vi: Điện thoại của tôi có vấn đề.
translations.nl: Ik heb een probleem met mijn telefoon.
translations.el: Έχω πρόβλημα με το τηλέφωνό μου.
translations.nb: Jeg har et problem med telefonen min.
translations.id: Saya punya masalah dengan ponsel saya.
translations.sv: Jag har problem med min telefon.
translations.ro: Am o problemă cu telefonul meu.
translations.da: Jeg har et problem med min telefon.
translations.ja: 携帯電話に問題があります。

tokens.explanation: 1st person singular personal pronoun in nominative; subject of the finite verb.
Main verb, present indicative, 1st person singular; expresses possession and selects an accusative object.
Indefinite article determiner; accusative neuter singular agreeing with the head noun 'Problem'.
Neuter noun; direct object in the accusative governed by 'habe'.
Preposition governing dative; introduces a prepositional complement.
Possessive determiner; dative neuter singular (1st person), agreeing with 'Handy'.
Neuter noun; object of the preposition 'mit' in the dative.

sentences.target: Das ist kein Problem für mich.

translations.de: Das ist kein Problem für mich.
translations.en: That’s not a problem for me.
translations.es: No es un problema para mí.
translations.fr: Ce n’est pas un problème pour moi.
translations.it: Per me non è un problema.
translations.pt: Para mim não é um problema.
translations.vi: Với tôi thì không thành vấn đề.
translations.nl: Dat is geen probleem voor mij.
translations.hi: यह मेरे लिए समस्या नहीं है.
translations.pl: To nie jest dla mnie problem.
translations.nb: Det er ikke noe problem for meg.
translations.sv: Det är inget problem för mig.
translations.ro: Nu e o problemă pentru mine.
translations.da: Det er ikke noget problem for mig.
translations.ja: 私には問題ありません。

tokens.explanation: Demonstrative pronoun; nominative neuter singular functioning as the subject.
Copular auxiliary, present indicative, 3rd person singular; links subject to predicate nominal.
Negative determiner; nominative neuter singular modifying 'Problem' to negate the NP.
Neuter noun; predicate nominal in nominative, complement of the copula 'ist'.
Preposition governing accusative; introduces a benefactive/affected participant.
Personal pronoun; accusative 1st person singular as object of 'für'.

sentences.target: Du hast ein Problem mit der Rechnung.
sentences.reading: /du hast aɪn pʁoˈbleːm mɪt deːɐ ˈʁɛçnʊŋ/

translations.de: Du hast ein Problem mit der Rechnung.
translations.ipa: /du hast aɪn pʁoˈbleːm mɪt deːɐ ˈʁɛçnʊŋ/
translations.en: You have a problem with the bill.
translations.es: Tienes un problema con la factura.
translations.fr: Tu as un problème avec la facture.
translations.it: Hai un problema con lo scontrino.
translations.pt: Você tem um problema com o recibo.
translations.vi: Bạn có vấn đề với hóa đơn.
translations.nl: Je hebt een probleem met het bonnetje.
translations.nb: Du har et problem med kvitteringen.
translations.sv: Du har ett problem med kvittot.
translations.da: Du har et problem med kvitteringen.
translations.ja: 請求書に問題があります。

tokens.explanation: 2nd person singular personal pronoun in nominative; subject of the finite verb.
Main verb, present indicative, 2nd person singular; takes an accusative object.
Indefinite article determiner; accusative neuter singular agreeing with 'Problem'.
Neuter noun; direct object in the accusative of 'hast'.
Preposition governing dative; introduces a prepositional complement.
Definite article; dative feminine singular agreeing with 'Rechnung'.
Feminine noun; object of 'mit' in the dative.

sentences.target: Es gibt ein Problem mit dem WLAN.
sentences.reading: /ɛs gɪpt aɪn pʁoˈbleːm mɪt deːm ˈveːlaːɛn/

translations.de: Es gibt ein Problem mit dem WLAN.
translations.ipa: /ɛs gɪpt aɪn pʁoˈbleːm mɪt deːm ˈveːlaːɛn/
translations.en: There is a problem with the Wi‑Fi.
translations.es: Hay un problema con el wifi.
translations.fr: Il y a un problème avec le Wi‑Fi.
translations.it: C’è un problema con il Wi‑Fi.
translations.pt: Há um problema com o Wi‑Fi.
translations.nl: Er is een probleem met de wifi.
translations.nb: Det er et problem med Wi‑Fi.
translations.da: Der er et problem med wifi.

tokens.explanation: Expletive/dummy pronoun; nominative neuter singular subject in the existential construction 'Es gibt'.
Main verb, present indicative, 3rd person singular; impersonal existential use selecting an accusative NP.
Indefinite article determiner; accusative neuter singular agreeing with 'Problem'.
Neuter noun; accusative object of the existential verb 'gibt'.
Preposition governing dative; introduces a prepositional complement.
Definite article; dative neuter singular agreeing with 'WLAN'.
Neuter common noun (acronym); object of 'mit' in the dative.
